section_name: Vehicles and Equipment Subject to Inspection

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A motor vehicle, trailer, semitrailer, pole trailer, or mobile home, registered in this state, must have the following items inspected at an inspection station or by an inspector:
safety guards or flaps, if required by Section 547.606 (Safety Guards or Flaps Required) ;
brake system, including power brake unit;
sunscreening devices, unless the vehicle is exempt from sunscreen device restrictions under Section 547.613 (Restrictions on Windows) ;
front seat belts in vehicles on which seat belt anchorages were part of the manufacturer’s original equipment;
fuel tank cap, using pressurized testing equipment approved by department rule;
emissions control equipment as designated by department rule; and
for purposes of Section 548.104 (Equipment-related Prerequisites to Issuance of Passing Vehicle Inspection Report) (d)(3), each license plate required to be displayed on the vehicle.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: A motor vehicle that uses electricity as its only source of motor power and that is not equipped with an internal combustion engine is exempt from the inspection requirements relating to the emissions and exhaust systems provided in Subsections (a)(12)-(15).
Repealed by Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 851 (H.B. 3297 ), Sec. 26(2), eff. January 1, 2025.

history.raw: Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 519 (H.B.3014), Sec. 1, eff. September 1, 2023.
